picks.name: Broadcom Inc.
picks.portfolioRole: Custom AI silicon and networking compounder
picks.thesisSummary: Broadcom remains the highest-priority long-term review because the stock is still in a constructive pullback while custom AI silicon, networking, and private-cloud inference demand all carry recent official confirmation.
picks.whyNow: The June 24 Jalapeno processor launch with OpenAI followed Broadcom's June quarter AI revenue acceleration, and the July 1 setup still offers a cleaner entry window than the prior vertical phase.
picks.valuationContext: The multiple is still premium, but the current setup is better supported than many AI peers because Broadcom has explicit AI revenue, customer programs, and cash generation to audit.
catalysts: Broadcom and OpenAI said on June 24 that they unveiled Jalapeno, an inference-oriented intelligence processor inside a multi-generation compute platform.
Broadcom reported fiscal Q2 AI semiconductor revenue of $10.8 billion and guided fiscal Q3 AI semiconductor revenue to $16.0 billion.
risks: Customer concentration: A meaningful share of upside still depends on a concentrated set of hyperscaler and model-builder AI programs.
AI upside still depends on a relatively concentrated hyperscaler and model-builder customer set.
watchItems: September earnings checkpoint: The next report needs to validate Broadcom's AI semiconductor revenue bridge into fiscal Q3.
Custom AI deployment breadth: Watch whether more production inference programs emerge beyond the current lead customers.
Change of view: A weakening AI revenue bridge, slower custom-accelerator deployments, or a failed September earnings follow-through would lower the priority.

evidence.title: OpenAI and Broadcom Unveil LLM-Optimized Intelligence Processor
Broadcom Inc. Announces Second Quarter Fiscal Year 2026 Financial Results

picks.name: Micron Technology, Inc.
picks.portfolioRole: Memory and storage AI cycle leader
picks.thesisSummary: Micron moved into the top tier because July 1 brought a fresh strategic auto-memory agreement only a week after record fiscal Q3 results confirmed the AI memory upcycle.
picks.whyNow: Micron's July 1 strategic agreement with General Motors gave the stock a same-week catalyst on top of record June-quarter results, keeping the AI-memory and edge-compute demand thesis highly current.
picks.valuationContext: The name is cyclical, but the reset is still compelling because record results, cash generation, and strategic supply commitments reduce the usual visibility discount.
catalysts: Micron and General Motors said on July 1 that they signed a strategic customer agreement to secure long-term supply of advanced memory and storage products.
Micron reported record fiscal third-quarter results on June 24 with strong data-center revenue, HBM momentum, and free cash flow.
risks: Memory-cycle volatility: Micron remains exposed to pricing and supply swings even when the current AI cycle is strong.
Micron remains structurally more cyclical than the software and platform leaders on today's list.
watchItems: July 21 dividend and customer-record date: Watch whether Micron's strategic customer agreement translates into more visible long-term design-win commentary by the next update cycle.
Next earnings reset: The next report needs to show that HBM, SSD, and data-center demand continue offsetting normal memory-cycle volatility.
Change of view: A reversal in HBM pricing, weaker data-center memory demand, or signs that record profitability was a short-lived peak would reduce the rank.

evidence.title: Micron and General Motors Sign Strategic Agreement to Secure Supply and Accelerate Innovation
Micron Technology, Inc. Reports Record Results for the Third Quarter of Fiscal 2026

picks.name: Vertiv Holdings Co
picks.portfolioRole: AI power and cooling infrastructure expansion
picks.thesisSummary: Vertiv stays near the top because July 1 delivered fresh manufacturing-capacity evidence that directly supports the AI cooling and power-density thesis.
picks.whyNow: Vertiv's July 1 Malaysia facility announcement added same-day operating evidence for AI infrastructure demand while the stock still screened as a constructive pullback.
picks.valuationContext: The multiple is rich, but the current price remains more defensible while management keeps translating AI demand into physical capacity expansion.
catalysts: Vertiv said on July 1 that it is expanding manufacturing capacity in Malaysia to support growing AI and digital-infrastructure demand across Asia.
VRT is scheduled to report on 2026-07-29 for the period ending 2026-06-30.
risks: Execution and valuation risk: Vertiv still needs to execute well against a premium multiple in a fast-scaling AI infrastructure market.
A premium industrial multiple leaves limited room for execution slippage.
watchItems: Late-July earnings checkpoint: The next report needs to confirm that manufacturing expansion is translating into delivery and backlog quality.
Regional supply-chain execution: Watch whether the new Malaysia facility improves supply resilience and deployment speed across Asia.
Change of view: A cooling-demand slowdown, manufacturing-ramp stumble, or renewed multiple expansion without earnings support would weaken the setup.

evidence.title: Vertiv Increases Manufacturing Capacity with New Facility in Malaysia

picks.name: NVIDIA Corporation
picks.thesisSummary: NVIDIA remains the cleanest direct AI infrastructure leader, and the latest pullback still offers a better review window than the prior melt-up phase.
picks.whyNow: NVIDIA's June 23 AWS production-at-scale update and July 1 capital-partner buildout message keep the AI factory narrative fresh into the late-August earnings checkpoint.
picks.valuationContext: NVIDIA is not optically cheap, but the current pullback is materially more interesting than the prior chase phase.
catalysts: NVIDIA said on July 1 that AI compute demand is shifting toward continuously operating AI factories and highlighted capital-partner support for the infrastructure buildout.
NVIDIA said on June 23 that it is working with AWS to bring AI to production at scale, reinforcing the AI factory deployment backdrop.
risks: Expectation and valuation risk: The business is elite, but market expectations still leave little room for an execution slip.
Valuation still assumes sustained AI capex strength.
watchItems: August earnings checkpoint: The next report needs to validate that the AI factory buildout is still broad and margin durable.
Production AI demand breadth: Watch whether the shift from experimentation to production inference broadens beyond the current hyperscaler leaders.
Change of view: A renewed melt-up without better price support, an earnings miss, or clear evidence that hyperscaler AI capex is normalizing would lower the priority.

evidence.title: NVIDIA Unlocks AI Compute at Scale, Inviting Capital Partners to Power the AI Infrastructure Buildout
NVIDIA and AWS Collaborate to Bring AI to Production at Scale

NVDA FMP quality and valuation snapshot

picks.name: Texas Instruments Incorporated
picks.thesisSummary: Texas Instruments stands out as a steadier semiconductor pullback with a fresh July 1 earnings-call catalyst and a still-discounted valuation versus its own quality profile.
picks.whyNow: TI refreshed the setup on July 1 by scheduling its Q2 earnings call while the stock remained in a constructive pullback, giving the analog recovery and capital-discipline thesis a near-term audit date.
picks.valuationContext: The stock is not outright cheap on every metric, but it looks more reasonable than many semiconductor peers given the balance-sheet resilience and pullback structure.
catalysts: TI said on July 1 that it will webcast its second-quarter 2026 earnings conference call on July 22.
TI said on June 2 that Julie Knecht will become CFO on August 1, 2026, while Rafael Lizardi retires after a transition period.
risks: Industrial analog recovery risk: TI still needs end-market demand to keep stabilizing for the rerating to continue.
The rerating still depends on a real analog demand recovery rather than just sentiment improvement.
watchItems: July 22 earnings call: The next earnings call needs to confirm demand stabilization and margin discipline.
Finance transition execution: Watch whether the CFO transition changes capital-allocation cadence or the messaging around the recovery.
Change of view: A weaker-than-expected analog recovery, acquisition-integration surprises, or a poor July earnings update would lower the rank.

evidence.title: Texas Instruments to webcast Q2 2026 earnings conference call
Texas Instruments announces CFO transition

picks.name: Cadence Design Systems, Inc.
picks.thesisSummary: Cadence earns a slot because the stock remains in a pullback while the June Intel Foundry and HPE updates kept the AI-design and AI-data-center software bottleneck thesis fresh.
picks.whyNow: Cadence has two recent official catalysts: the June 8 Intel Foundry process-optimization collaboration and the June 16 HPE digital-twin update for AI data-center modernization.
picks.valuationContext: Cadence still trades at a premium, but the current pullback is easier to underwrite because the software bottleneck remains scarce and recent partnership evidence is tangible.
catalysts: Cadence said on June 8 that it expanded collaboration with Intel Foundry to accelerate 14A process optimization for HPC and mobile designs.
Cadence said on June 16 that it expanded collaboration with HPE to accelerate digital twin-driven AI data-center modernization.
risks: Premium multiple risk: Cadence carries a premium software multiple, so a good business can still de-rate if AI design demand slows.
The multiple remains premium and can compress if demand signals soften.
watchItems: Late-July earnings checkpoint: The next report needs to show that AI-driven EDA demand and data-center modernization spending remain healthy.
Change of view: A failure to translate AI design complexity into backlog acceleration or a renewed valuation expansion without estimate support would reduce the appeal.

evidence.title: Cadence Announces Collaboration with Intel Foundry to Accelerate Intel 14A Process Optimization
Cadence Accelerates Digital Twin-Driven Data Center AI Modernization with HPE

picks.name: VanEck Semiconductor ETF
picks.portfolioRole: Semiconductor basket instead of extra single-name concentration
picks.thesisSummary: SMH makes the list as the best diversified semiconductor reset after the July 1 close, letting the portfolio keep AI-chip exposure without over-concentrating in one additional single name.
picks.whyNow: The ETF still sits in a constructive pullback while major holdings keep providing fresh official AI-demand evidence, including July 1 NVIDIA infrastructure commentary and July 1 Micron strategic supply news.
picks.valuationContext: The basket is less valuation-specific than a single name, but the pullback plus diversified exposure makes it a useful long-term entry alternative to chasing every semiconductor winner individually.
catalysts: VanEck's SMH product page showed $73.28 billion of net assets, 72.18% year-to-date return, and a 0.35% expense ratio as of July 1, 2026.
NVIDIA said on July 1 that compute demand is shifting toward continuously operating AI factories, reinforcing the core semiconductor-demand backdrop for SMH.
risks: Theme concentration: SMH is still concentrated in the semiconductor theme and can remain volatile when AI sentiment shifts.
The ETF is still concentrated in a single cyclical theme even if single-name risk is reduced.
watchItems: Semiconductor earnings season: Watch whether major holdings keep validating AI demand and margin durability through July and August results.
Change of view: If the semiconductor basket re-extends without fresh estimate support or if AI infrastructure demand visibly broadens less than expected, the ETF should move lower in priority.

evidence.title: VanEck Semiconductor ETF product page
NVIDIA Unlocks AI Compute at Scale, Inviting Capital Partners to Power the AI Infrastructure Buildout

picks.name: Digital Realty Trust, Inc.
picks.portfolioRole: Data-center REIT with AI land-and-power optionality
picks.thesisSummary: Digital Realty remains one of the cleaner non-chip AI infrastructure names because recent land, capital, and earnings-calendar evidence still supports hyperscale expansion while the stock stays in a pullback.
picks.whyNow: Digital Realty refreshed the setup on June 30 by scheduling Q2 earnings, and the recent Kansas City land and Singapore AI expansion evidence still supports a source-backed why-now.
picks.valuationContext: The REIT is not cheap in absolute terms, but the AI-campus optionality and pullback timing still make the current review window attractive.
catalysts: Digital Realty said on June 30 that it scheduled its second-quarter 2026 earnings release and conference call.
Digital Realty said it is targeting nearly S$7 billion of investment to strengthen Singapore as a production-scale AI base.
risks: Rate and capital intensity risk: Data-center REITs remain sensitive to funding conditions and large development commitments.
Funding conditions and large development commitments can still pressure the multiple.
watchItems: July earnings checkpoint: The next report needs to show that hyperscale AI demand is still translating into leasing and development quality.
Change of view: A capital-markets wobble, weaker leasing pace, or a less convincing hyperscale-demand message at the next earnings update would reduce the priority.

evidence.title: Digital Realty Schedules Second Quarter 2026 Earnings Release and Conference Call
Digital Realty Targets Nearly S$7 Billion Investment to Strengthen Singapore as a Production-Scale AI Base

picks.name: RTX Corporation
picks.thesisSummary: RTX keeps the single defense slot because recent contract and earnings-schedule evidence still make it the most current large-cap rearmament setup.
picks.whyNow: The June 26 AIM-9X award and June 30 earnings-date release keep defense production follow-through current just ahead of the July 7-8 NATO summit.
picks.valuationContext: RTX is more reasonably valued than several aerospace peers while still participating in the current rearmament cycle.
catalysts: RTX said on June 26 that Raytheon won a $1.1 billion U.S. Navy contract to produce AIM-9X Block II missiles for U.S. and allied customers.
RTX said on June 30 that it will release second-quarter 2026 earnings on July 23 before the market opens.
risks: Program execution and budget risk: Defense programs still depend on procurement pacing and large-program execution.
Defense budgets and multi-year programs can still move unevenly even when the long-term trend is favorable.
watchItems: July 23 earnings call: The next report needs to confirm defense backlog quality and cash generation remain intact.
NATO summit follow-through: Watch whether the July 7-8 NATO summit reinforces rearmament commitments relevant to missile and air-defense demand.
Change of view: A weaker-than-expected backlog or cash-flow message, or evidence that defense-spending momentum is fading, would lower the score.

evidence.title: RTX's Raytheon awarded $1.1 billion U.S. Navy contract to produce AIM-9X Block II missiles

picks.name: Intuitive Surgical, Inc.
picks.portfolioRole: Healthcare quality compounder on a reset
picks.thesisSummary: Intuitive stays in the top ten because the stock remains well off prior highs while procedure growth, da Vinci 5 adoption, and the July 16 earnings date keep the healthcare quality thesis reviewable.
picks.whyNow: The shares still trade far below prior highs even after strong April results, and the July 16 Q2 earnings call is the next clear checkpoint for whether procedure growth and da Vinci 5 momentum remain intact.
picks.valuationContext: The valuation is still premium, but the pullback is deep enough to reopen the name for long-term review.
catalysts: Intuitive said on April 21 that first-quarter 2026 results reflected strong procedure growth and continued adoption of the da Vinci 5 platform.
Intuitive's investor relations site lists the Q2 2026 earnings conference call for July 16, 2026.
risks: Valuation and competition risk: The business is strong, but valuation and competitive positioning still need continued execution support.
The stock still trades at a premium and needs continued execution to justify a rerating.
watchItems: July 16 earnings call: The next report needs to confirm da Vinci 5 adoption and procedure growth remain strong enough to support the rerating case.
Change of view: A slowdown in procedure growth, weaker da Vinci 5 placements, or a disappointing July earnings update would reduce the score.
